[bd prime] If this output is truncated by your host, read the full persisted hook output before continuing; it may contain project memories and session rules not visible in the preview.

# Beads Workflow Context

> **Context Recovery**: Run `bd prime` after compaction, clear, or new session
> Hooks auto-call this in Claude Code when a beads workspace is resolved

# 🚨 SESSION CLOSE PROTOCOL 🚨

**CRITICAL**: Before saying "done" or "complete", you MUST run this checklist:

```
[ ] 1. git status              (check what changed)
[ ] 2. git add <files>         (stage code changes)
[ ] 3. git commit -m "..."     (commit code)
[ ] 4. git push                (push to remote)
```

**NEVER skip this.** Work is not done until pushed.

## Core Rules
- **Default**: Use beads for ALL task tracking (`bd create`, `bd ready`, `bd close`)
- **Prohibited**: Do NOT use TodoWrite, TaskCreate, or markdown files for task tracking
- **Workflow**: Create beads issue BEFORE writing code, mark in_progress when starting
- **Memory**: Use `bd remember "insight"` for persistent knowledge across sessions. Do NOT use MEMORY.md files — they fragment across accounts. Search with `bd memories <keyword>`.
- Persistence you don't need beats lost context
- Git workflow: beads auto-commit to Dolt, run `git push` at session end
- Session management: check `bd ready` for available work

## Essential Commands

### Finding Work
- `bd ready` - Show issues ready to work (no blockers)
- `bd list --status=open` - All open issues
- `bd list --status=in_progress` - Your active work
- `bd show <id>` - Detailed issue view with dependencies

### Creating & Updating
- `bd create --title="Summary of this issue" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task|bug|feature --priority=2` - New issue
  - Priority: 0-4 or P0-P4 (0=critical, 2=medium, 4=backlog). NOT "high"/"medium"/"low"
- `bd update <id> --claim` - Claim work
- `bd update <id> --assignee=username` - Assign to someone
- `bd update <id> --title/--description/--notes/--design` - Update fields inline
- `bd close <id>` - Mark complete
- `bd close <id1> <id2> ...` - Close multiple issues at once (more efficient)
- `bd close <id> --reason="explanation"` - Close with reason
- **Tip**: When creating multiple issues/tasks/epics, use parallel subagents for efficiency
- **WARNING**: Do NOT use `bd edit` - it opens $EDITOR (vim/nano) which blocks agents

### Dependencies & Blocking
- `bd dep add <issue> <depends-on>` - Add dependency (issue depends on depends-on)
- `bd blocked` - Show all blocked issues
- `bd show <id>` - See what's blocking/blocked by this issue

### Sync & Collaboration
- `bd dolt push` - Push beads to Dolt remote
- `bd dolt pull` - Pull beads from Dolt remote
- `bd search <query>` - Search issues by keyword

### Project Health
- `bd stats` - Project statistics (open/closed/blocked counts)
- `bd doctor` - Check for issues (sync problems, missing hooks)
- `bd doctor --check=conventions` - Check for convention drift (lint, stale, orphans)

### Quality Tools
- `bd create --validate` - Check description has required sections
- `bd create --acceptance="criteria"` - Set acceptance criteria (checked by --validate)
- `bd create --design="decisions"` - Record design decisions
- `bd create --notes="context"` - Add supplementary notes
- `bd config set validation.on-create warn` - Auto-validate on every create
- `bd lint` - Check existing issues for missing sections

### Lifecycle & Hygiene
- `bd defer <id> --until="date"` - Defer work to a future date
- `bd supersede <id> --with=<new-id>` - Mark issue as superseded
- `bd close <id> --suggest-next` - Show newly unblocked issues after closing
- `bd stale` - Find issues with no recent activity
- `bd orphans` - Find issues with broken dependencies
- `bd preflight` - Pre-PR checks (lint, stale, orphans)
- `bd human <id>` - Flag for human decision (list/respond/dismiss)

### Structured Workflows
- `bd formula list` - See available workflow templates
- `bd mol pour <name>` - Start structured workflow from formula

## Common Workflows

**Starting work:**
```bash
bd ready           # Find available work
bd show <id>       # Review issue details
bd update <id> --claim  # Claim it
```

**Completing work:**
```bash
bd close <id1> <id2> ...    # Close all completed issues at once
git add . && git commit -m "..."  # Commit code changes
git push                    # Push to remote
```

**Creating dependent work:**
```bash
# Run bd create commands in parallel (use subagents for many items)
bd create --title="Implement feature X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=feature
bd create --title="Write tests for X" --description="Why this issue exists and what needs to be done" --type=task
bd dep add beads-yyy beads-xxx  # Tests depend on Feature (Feature blocks tests)
```

# FABLE INTERPRETATION — f2b-transfer STAGE-1 (mechanical: endorsement kill (d) did NOT fire; A = 0.978, one-sided 95% Wilson LB = 0.961; instrument: VALID; experiment state: stage-1 passed → INCOMPLETE-DATA, stage 2 licensed)

- **Author:** Fable (chief architect), 2026-07-11. This is the FABLE interpretive reading of
  the f2b-transfer STAGE-1 adjudication result. The coordinator computed the mechanical
  result; I own the epistemic reading. This document changes NO frozen record, NO verdict
  object, NO log, NO analysis output, and NO registered assumption; it runs no experiment,
  no git, no bd, no kb-sync.
- **Sources (read at source, in full):** `registry/experiments/f2b-transfer.json` (FROZEN,
  frozen_sha256 b341a090…, esp. H-TRANSFER/H-CIRC, kill_criterion_verbatim clause (d), and
  `extrapolation_envelope_verbatim` — BINDING on every line below),
  `poc/f2b-transfer/judge-1-results/stage1-analysis.json` (the coordinator's mechanical
  stage-1 result, restated verbatim, never recomputed), `poc/f2b-transfer/design.md`
  (prereg_doc, sha-pinned in the record; §2, §4 incl. correction-1 standards S1–S7, §7,
  §8, §10), and `docs/next/feasibility-synthesis.md` (the capstone, 2026-07-11).
- **Epistemic discipline:** every load-bearing line carries its `[TAG]` on the same logical
  line. **MEASURED** restates a measured/computed fact strictly within this record's own
  envelope; **STIPULATED** marks framing and frozen-design rulings adopted verbatim;
  **EXTRAPOLATION** is direction-only and never load-bearing. No number here is recomputed;
  every figure is the coordinator's or the frozen record's, cited.

---

## 0. The mechanical facts, restated inside the envelope

PREMISE: [MEASURED: poc/f2b-transfer/judge-1-results/stage1-analysis.json] Over the 360 blind d-qa-t items, judge-1 (a kernel-naive HUMAN) and judge-2 (the pre-declared cross-vendor fallback, GPT-5.5, temperature 0, pinned prompt, per protocol §4.1) returned concordant labels on 324/360 — raw judge-pair agreement 0.90 ≥ the 0.80 G-adj bar. Concordant escapes: ZERO (n_undecided = 0) — no item was concordantly judged CONTENT-UNDECIDED. Over the 324 resolved-by-concordance items, blind external gold equals the kernel's MEMBERSHIP gold on 317 → external endorsement A = 317/324 = 0.978, one-sided 95% Wilson LB = 0.961.

PREMISE: [MEASURED: same source — the gate and the kill] `adjudication_valid` = TRUE on every pre-registered G-adj clause (n_adjudicated 360 ≥ 300; unresolved 36 = 10% ≤ 15%; agreement 0.90 ≥ 0.80) and `stage1_endorsement_fail` = FALSE: the Wilson LB 0.961 sits far above the 0.70 kill bar. Kill (d) — the pre-GPU FAIL evaluable from the stage-1 record alone — did NOT fire.

PREMISE: [MEASURED: coordinator's robustness bound, stated with the mechanical result; not recomputed here] The 36 discordant judge-1/judge-2 pairs are currently UNRESOLVED (the judge-3 human tie-break has not yet run) and are counted per the frozen formula as excluded from A's denominator. The endorsement conclusion is ROBUST to their resolution: in the worst case (all 36 resolve against membership) A = 317/360 = 0.881, Wilson LB ≈ 0.84 — still far above the 0.70 kill bar. No judge-3 outcome can fire kill (d).

PREMISE: [MEASURED: registry/experiments/f2b-transfer.json verdict_rules + design.md §7] With rule 1 (G-adj INSTRUMENT-INVALID) not matching and rule 2 (stage-1 FAIL) not matching, and every stage-2 field unset, the lawful mechanical state of the experiment is **INCOMPLETE-DATA** — stage-1 passed, fields for later rules unset, fail closed. This is the frozen record's own named intermediate state, and it is the state that LICENSES stage 2.

PREMISE: [MEASURED: m0b, restated mandatorily per the envelope] Kernel-expressibility coverage 0.3542 at rung molecules-v0, measured on one incomplete kernel-v0 instance — NOT general coverage. Every claim below is bounded to the kernel-covered slice.

---

## 1. What the stage-1 pass adjudicates: H-TRANSFER vs H-CIRC

LOAD-BEARING (the central reading, resting on the §0 MEASURED facts read against the frozen hypothesis text): H-CIRC's sharpest, cheapest, pre-registered prediction is REFUTED at this kernel instance and rung. The frozen record gave H-CIRC a zero-GPU falsification route: if the kernel records are "an arbitrary-but-self-consistent codebook", blind judges who have never read them — answering from ordinary competence with the covered everyday concepts, with a mandatory cannot-say escape — should scatter or escape, driving A near option-chance/claim-base-rate and firing kill (d) before a dollar of GPU is spent [STIPULATED: H-CIRC verbatim, hypotheses[1] + design.md §2]. The observed result is not merely above the bar; it is near ceiling: A = 0.978 with Wilson LB 0.961 against a 0.70 kill bar, and the escape hatch H-CIRC needed — built in precisely so that "the kernel's content does not describe this concept" would be expressible — fired concordantly on ZERO of 360 items [MEASURED: §0]. A kernel-naive human, blind to provenance, membership gold, and record structure, independently reproduced the kernel's own gold labels on essentially the entire concordant set. An arbitrary codebook does not survive that contact [STIPULATED: the design's own §2 contrast, applied to the observed branch].

LOAD-BEARING (the symmetric honesty): H-CIRC is NOT fully dead, because the frozen hypothesis is disjunctive — "A near chance … **and/or** no external-gold lift (kill a)" [STIPULATED: hypotheses[1] verbatim]. Stage 1 kills the CONTENT-level circularity reading (the codebook story). The SYSTEM-level residual — whether the verifier-retry LIFT survives re-scoring against this externally-fixed gold — is exactly what the stage-2 GPU arms exist to measure, and kills (a), (b), (c) all remain live. The design's own decision table (design.md §10, row "A high, primary fails") pre-names the outcome where content is externally endorsed but the system lift does not transfer; nothing in stage 1 forecloses it [STIPULATED: design.md §10; §2 layer-2 "measure, don't extrapolate"].

LOAD-BEARING (what stage 1 is evidence FOR): H-TRANSFER's content premise — "the kernel records carry semantic content that is true of the covered concepts as externally understood" — now has direct, blind, human-anchored measured support at this instance, on this slice [MEASURED: A = 0.978 within the envelope; STIPULATED: the H-TRANSFER wording, design.md §1]. Every one of the 317 agreements carries a kernel-naive HUMAN's blind label (concordance construction: the resolved label IS judge-1's label wherever it counts), so this is categorically stronger than the f2b-transfer-llmproxy stand-in (A_1p = 0.95, single LLM judge family, weak proxy per ASM-0022): the proxy's letter-level circularity break is now a human-anchored one [MEASURED: stage1-analysis.json; capstone §2 for the proxy's status; the numeric proxy-vs-human differencing is left to the coordinator against the pinned comparator — direction-consistency only is noted here, EXTRAPOLATION].

INTERPRETIVE NOTE (direction-only, never a premise): with A = 0.978, external and membership gold nearly coincide on the labelled set, which makes a LARGE dual-scoring gap (lift_mem ≫ lift_ext) arithmetically unlikely on the stage-2 eval prefix — but the frozen design explicitly forbids inferring the primary from A ("retry dynamics on the judge–kernel disagreement set are not a pure function of A"; promotion requires the measured system-level result) [EXTRAPOLATION, bounded by STIPULATED: design.md §2 layer 2]. Stage 2 is cheap (~$1 point estimate, caps $15/4 GPU-h) and is the only lawful way to convert this endorsement into a transfer claim [STIPULATED: design.md §9].

---

## 2. What a stage-1 endorsement pass LICENSES — and what it does NOT

**Licensed, exactly this and no more:**

- LOAD-BEARING: it removes the gold-definition-circularity confound — the specific residual f2b-replicate's assessment foregrounded ("content-specificity ≠ ground-truth independence") — for THIS kernel instance (kernel-v0/molecules-v0 as pinned) at this rung of item surface: kernel-covered, kernel-RENDERED templated definitional QA over the 108 covered concepts, under THIS gold standard (blind dual-judge adjudication per the pinned d-adj-t protocol, judge sourcing disclosed: human judge-1 + pre-declared GPT-5.5 fallback judge-2) [MEASURED: A/LB within extrapolation_envelope_verbatim; STIPULATED: envelope clause "A PASS removes exactly ONE confound … gold defined by the kernel's own string-equality loses its gold-definition leg"]. There now exists an external gold authority — ordinary human understanding, blind to the kernel — and the kernel's membership gold agrees with it at 0.978 on this slice. "Gold := whatever the kernel says" is no longer the only reading of the F2-line gold.
- LOAD-BEARING: it licenses STAGE 2 — the lawful INCOMPLETE-DATA state permits the GPU arms to run under the frozen stage discipline (reuse-check → dry-plan → mock → single GPU run) [MEASURED: registry/experiments/f2b-transfer.json stage_discipline + verdict_rules (INCOMPLETE-DATA the lawful intermediate state licensing stage 2, restated verbatim); design.md §7/§11 steps 4–6].
- It retires, for this design, the cheapest FAIL route: the experiment can no longer end at ~$0; its remaining outcomes are decided by the stage-2 arms [MEASURED: kill (d) evaluable-from-stage-1-alone, not fired; robust per §0].

**NOT licensed — each item within the binding envelope, restated without widening [STIPULATED: extrapolation_envelope_verbatim, clause by clause]:**

- It does NOT establish the stage-2 verifier-offload lift on external gold. The primary (acc_ext(135M+kernel-verify-retry, k=4) − acc_ext(135M-alone)), the shuffled-content control (kill b), the gloss-commodity control (kill c), the non-inferiority-vs-1.7B headline, and every instrument gate on the GPU arms (engagement, headroom, separation, P10) are UNRUN. No verifier-offload promotion beyond the self-authored slice is licensed by A, however high [STIPULATED: design.md §2 layer 2; the envelope binds any PASS, and there is no PASS yet — the mechanical state is INCOMPLETE-DATA].
- It does NOT license external question-STYLE ecological validity. The item surface stays kernel-rendered templated QA; public-benchmark surfaces remain unmeasured for system lift, and measurably so — the deterministic verifier cannot engage them (f2b-replicate d-ext: verify/alone byte-identical at every seed) [MEASURED: the record's own d-ext assumption; STIPULATED: envelope]. The constrained IF-C answer surface remains and stays disclosed.
- It does NOT license any coverage-general claim: coverage 0.3542 at molecules-v0, one incomplete kernel-v0 instance, restated mandatorily [MEASURED: m0b; STIPULATED: envelope].
- It does NOT license any benchmark-style, PRM-class, or scale claim: HC3 stays indexed to the 1.5B class of f2b-replicate; two host rungs license a SIGN, not a slope; nothing beyond ≤1.7B hosts and the fixed k=4 budget [STIPULATED: envelope].
- It does NOT bear on the NL/reachability boundary at all: no natural-language input, no parser, and no host model appears anywhere in stage 1. The l3a-parse/a5-nl FAILs stand untouched; a deployment path from natural input to this formal slice remains negative at scope [MEASURED: capstone §1 C2, each figure index-bound; STIPULATED: this framing].

**Instrument honesty carried with the result:**

- Judge-2 was the pre-declared LLM fallback, not a second human. This was frozen in advance with E5 leak-checked-judge discipline and must be disclosed in every readout; the human anchor of the result is judge-1, present in every resolved label [STIPULATED: protocol §4.1; MEASURED: sourcing as recorded].
- The correction-1 adjudication standards (S1/S4/S6) RAISE A by removing minimality- and style-driven escapes, with a frozen argument that no H-CIRC signal is lost (under H-CIRC the clauses are false/misfitting and the disagreeing label still forces through) and with the A-direction of every rule disclosed pre-data [STIPULATED: design.md §4.7, adopted verbatim — a pre-data clarification, not a post-hoc patch; the two accepted honest costs (contentless segments lose credit; genuinely-shared donor content counts against, ~1% order) both push A DOWN, and A still reads 0.978].
- The 36 discordant pairs (10%) are within the 15% instrument cap and cannot move the verdict [MEASURED: §0]. GOVERNANCE NOTE (sequencing, not a contradiction of any number): the frozen protocol (§4.4–4.5) runs the judge-3 tie-break BEFORE d-adj-t is hash-pinned and the formal stage-1 record appended, and the stage-2 eval set is the deterministic 250-prefix of the externally-labelled items — judge-3's resolutions can add labels and therefore fix the prefix composition. The ~15-minute judge-3 round should complete before the d-adj-t pin; the epistemic reading above is invariant to its outcome (worst-case A = 0.881), but the formal record append should follow the frozen order [STIPULATED: protocol §4.5; recommendation to the coordinator/runner, no record modified here].

---

## 3. CAPSTONE DELTA — recommendation only (do NOT edit feasibility-synthesis.md here)

DECISION (recommendation only, applied by the coordinator or a later synthesis pass): the capstone (`docs/next/feasibility-synthesis.md`, 2026-07-11) should incorporate the stage-1 result as follows [STIPULATED: consistent with the capstone's maintenance discipline under ASM-0760/0761]:

1. **Situate it precisely: the FIRST strong measured POSITIVE crossing into ground-truth-INDEPENDENT gold — on the gold-authority axis, not the input axis.** The capstone's governing boundary line ("every MEASURED crossing of a boundary into real or natural INPUT either FAILs or is INCONCLUSIVE", ASM-0762) SURVIVES VERBATIM: stage 1 crossed a different boundary — who fixes the gold — while the input surface stayed kernel-rendered. Add a distinct, disclaimed companion line, not a revision of ASM-0762: *the first time the kernel's own content was scored against an authority it did not define — a blind kernel-naive human — it was endorsed at A = 0.978 (Wilson LB 0.961, n=324 resolved of 360; robust worst-case 0.881 pending judge-3)*. This is the precise contrast with the NL-boundary FAILs: those measured whether outside input can REACH the kernel (no, at scope); this measured whether the kernel's content is TRUE by outside lights when reached (yes, at 0.978, on this slice). One axis negative, the other now positive; neither reads onto the other [EXTRAPOLATION: the axis contrast, direction-only, never a premise].
2. **§2 efficiency — update the f2b-replicate confound paragraph.** The LOAD-BEARING line "the only experiments that could settle it — the human f2b-transfer Stage-1, or the K-NULL ablation — are unrun" is now stale: the human Stage-1 HAS read out, positively, at the content level. Replace with: the gold-definition-circularity leg of the f2b-replicate confound is REMOVED at this kernel instance (stage-1 A = 0.978, LB 0.961, human-anchored, kill (d) not fired); the remaining opens are (i) the SYSTEM-level transfer of the lift on external gold — the stage-2 GPU arms, now the cheapest verdict-moving compute in the registry (~$1 point estimate, $15 cap, no longer human-blocked) — and (ii) the alignment-vs-content attribution (knull), which stage 1 does NOT touch: a blind judge endorsing the records' truth says nothing about whether a generic aligned answer-key would produce the same lift.
3. **§3 ledger — split the f2b-transfer row.** Move "f2b-transfer (human judge-1) | FROZEN-UNRUN, human-blocked" to: stage-1 adjudication DONE (endorsement PASS, A 0.978/LB 0.961, adjudication_valid TRUE, mechanical state INCOMPLETE-DATA; judge-3 tie-break ~15 min pending, verdict-robust); stage-2 GPU arms PENDING, **compute-blocked only** (~$1). Reversal power stays "Verdict-moving" — but the mover is now stage 2, and its blocking constraint has flipped from human annotation to a trivial GPU run. The capstone's bottom-line claim "the binding constraint on both verdicts is not compute — it is human annotation" needs a carve-out for this line: its human constraint is now (nearly) discharged.
4. **The efficiency verdict does NOT move off INCONCLUSIVE-PENDING — and must not, yet.** Four reasons, each frozen: (i) the mechanical state is INCOMPLETE-DATA, not PASS — no end-task number on external gold exists; §0's governing sentence ("ZERO audited end-task wins … attributable to kernel content") stands verbatim, since stage 1 is not an end-task win (no host model, no task); (ii) the capstone's pending leg (a) was disjunctive across TWO confounds and stage 1 discharges only the gold-circularity half — knull (content vs generic-aligned-key) is untouched and unrun; (iii) legs (b) mint-cost (A-F0, key-gated) and (c) consumption channel (A-E2) are untouched; (iv) the envelope forbids reading A as a transfer result. What DOES change is the verdict's TEXTURE and its expected resolution path: H-CIRC lost its cheapest support and its zero-GPU kill route; the standing INCONCLUSIVE is now one short, cheap, pre-registered GPU run away from becoming either the programme's first ground-truth-independent end-task PASS (if the primary and controls hold) or a sharp, well-scoped FAIL (kill a: content endorsed, system lift doesn't transfer). The honest capstone phrasing: *"the efficiency thesis's decisive experiment is no longer human-blocked; its content premise just survived its first blind human contact at 0.978"* [EXTRAPOLATION: direction-only, never a premise].
5. **Correctness thesis — direction-only colour, NO verdict movement.** Stage 1 is registered on the efficiency line, but it is also the first blind HUMAN endorsement of kernel explication content at the whole-item level (S1 fit+identification, S4 generic standard) — a positive datum on the same content-validity flank where g3-llmproxy-v3 just posted a clause-literal proxy WARNING (concordant necessity-violation 0.1846). These do not contradict: different instruments, different grain (whole-explication endorsement vs clause-wise literal necessity), one human-anchored and one LLM-proxy; both await their full human adjudicators (judge-3/stage-2 here; GATE-H g3 there). Recommend recording the pair as the two current, non-contradictory readings of the content-validity axis, neither reading onto the other's endpoint [EXTRAPOLATION: direction-only; the correctness verdict's two decisive legs (g2, NL parser) are untouched and it stays INCONCLUSIVE-PENDING].

---

## 4. Epistemic register (what this interpretation relied on)

- **MEASURED, never widened, never recomputed:** every count, rate, and bound in §0 (A = 317/324 = 0.978; Wilson LB 0.961; agreement 0.90 = 324/360; n_undecided 0; unresolved 36 = 10%; adjudication_valid TRUE; stage1_endorsement_fail FALSE; worst-case 317/360 = 0.881, LB ≈ 0.84 — the coordinator's robustness bound, restated), each strictly inside the frozen record's extrapolation_envelope_verbatim; coverage 0.3542 (m0b); the f2b-replicate d-ext byte-identity fact, cited at its own envelope.
- **STIPULATED, adopted verbatim from the frozen record and prereg_doc:** the H-TRANSFER/H-CIRC wording and their disjunctive structure; kill (d) and its A formula; the G-adj gate; the "measure, don't extrapolate" layer-2 ruling; the correction-1 standards and their disclosed A-direction; the judge-2 fallback discipline; the envelope's licensed/not-licensed clauses; the INCOMPLETE-DATA stage discipline; the judge-3-before-pin protocol order.
- **EXTRAPOLATION, direction-only, load-bearing for nothing:** the "arithmetically unlikely large dual-scoring gap" note (§1); the proxy-vs-human direction-consistency note (§1); the gold-authority-axis vs input-axis contrast and every capstone-delta phrasing in §3; the g3-proxy juxtaposition (§3.5).

---

item.text: The first pass shows the document is disciplined about the major scope boundaries, but several load-bearing phrasings need exact checking against the registry—notably “refuted,” “ordinary human understanding,” “entire concordant set,” and the robustness/Wilson claim.    201	## Epistemic register (what this synthesis relied on)
   202	
   203	- **STIPULATED (ASM-0760):** the two standing per-thesis feasibility verdicts and their scopes/confidences (the capstone decision) — both INCONCLUSIVE-PENDING within the stated envelopes.
   204	- **STIPULATED (ASM-0761):** no in-flight item (g3-llmproxy-v3, knull plain-arm rewrite) is verdict-moving; the standing verdict is authored now, not deferred.
   205	- **EXTRAPOLATION (ASM-0762, never a premise):** the reachability-wall-on-coverage-wall cross-thesis reading; each constituent figure is index-bound to its own verdict's envelope.
   206	- **STIPULATED, adopted verbatim from the source assessments:** the f2b lift is correct-alignment-specific not kernel-content-specific; A_1p 0.95 is a WEAK proxy (ASM-0022); the A-E2 figure is a membership upper bound; the truthstyle PASS is instrument-only (ASM-0680); the knull surface-realization gap is a named unmeasured component (ASM-0702).
   207	- **MEASURED, fully indexed and never widened:** every §1–§2 number carries its corpus/rung/kernel-state/vertical indices verbatim; m0b 0.3542 is restated only as a corpus-indexed gate, never as "natural coverage".
   208	
   209	This is the definitive standing feasibility verdict on the current evidence. It moves from
   210	INCONCLUSIVE toward FEASIBLE or NOT-FEASIBLE only when the verdict-moving PENDING items
   211	(§3) read out; it changes no frozen object, no verdict, and no audit.

  "H-CIRC (the alternative under test, killed or supported symmetrically): the F2-line lift exists only because d-qa/d-qa-r gold was DEFINED by the same string-equality the verifier checks; predicts blind-endorsement A near chance (stage-1 kill d fires with zero GPU spend) and/or no external-gold lift (kill a)",
  "H-STRUCT carry-over: any external-gold lift must remain kernel-CONTENT-specific — the seed-pinned shuffled-record derangement at identical retry topology and cost recovers <30% of it (kill b otherwise)",
  "RT-2 carry-over on external gold: the kernel arm cannot PASS without beating gloss-text self-verify+retry at matched budget and FLOPs (kill c otherwise)"
]

WHAT IT DOES NOT GENERALIZE TO: (a) NO other mapper, gazetteer, or frame build — this is an a1-hybrid-specific result and the pre-declared successor (FK-NLB-3) is an LLM-assisted parser, a DIFFERENT front-end class the kill routes investment to; (b) NO other codebase or programming language, NO other phrasing distribution, NO natural-user-distribution robustness claim (phrasings agent-authored under a blind protocol); NO extraction-soundness claim w.r.t. runtime semantics (ASM-0009 unchanged); (c) NO LLM-comparative accuracy or cost claim (a5-llm / a5-llm-frontier), NO statement about kernel usefulness to any model; (d) NO cross-vertical conjunction — l3a-parse licenses the family/world vertical separately, and its fail-closed OUTCOME is the OPPOSITE (its S2 KILL did not fire), so the safety reading must stay strictly per-vertical; (e) it does NOT indict the kernel, the engine, or the closed grammar — the gold path replicates perfectly (855/855), so this is a boundary/front-end result, not a decidability result. The margin +/-0.10 is a STIPULATED equivalence threshold (design section 4.3, FORK-5), and the measured effect is real: 0.025 pooled with an interval excluding zero, NSM-favoring, concentrated in the truth-correct cell (point 0.05) and largest for the designer-family judge (p3 Haiku 0.045 vs p1 0.01, p2 0.02). registry/assessments/truthstyle-2x2.json:74:  "composition_note": "Where this sits in the correctness-thesis evidence tree. The thesis currently rests on two live lines plus their instruments: (1) the f2b line (verifier-offload): f2b-replicate PROMOTED at envelope scope; f2b-transfer frozen and unconsumed (the sole H-TRANSFER vs H-CIRC adjudicator); f2b-transfer-llmproxy A_1p=0.95 as a weak feasibility proxy (ASM-0022 ceiling); (2) the NL-boundary line (l3a-parse / a5-llm): grammar reachability and the engine-vs-LLM differentiator, with its own audit custody. truthstyle-2x2 is f2b-line-LOCAL: it is a NECESSARY SUPPORTING result - a style-robustness certificate for the judge instrument - not a direct kernel-correctness claim. Necessary in the precise sense that without it every f2b-line LLM-judge endorsement statistic carried an unquantified style channel (the guard this record's consumer rule governed), and the llmproxy assessment could cite A_1p=0.95 only with that channel explicitly open; with it, the channel is bounded at 0.10 (measured 0.025) for the pool actually in force. Supporting in the precise sense that it scores zero kernel content: were every kernel gloss wrong, this record could read identically (a style-robust judge is an instrument fact). It contributes to the CORRECTNESS thesis by making the f2b line's endorsement evidence INTERPRETABLE, and it was one of the three pre-unblind obligations the llmproxy assessment named (truthstyle-2x2; knull + content-injection map; failure-cause taxonomy) - it has now landed WITH confirmatory standing, having frozen 2026-07-10T10:02:06Z before f2b-transfer Stage-2 unblinds (R-2 timing honoured). It neither strengthens nor constrains the NL-boundary records: l3a/a5 consume no LLM-judge endorsement reading of kernel-register text, so the guard-lift does not reach them and their results do not back-stop this one. The load-bearing chain for the eventual correctness verdict now reads: judge instrument style-clean at margin (THIS RECORD) -> proxy endorsement interpretable (llmproxy, weak-proxy ceiling) -> human Stage-1 adjudicates H-TRANSFER vs H-CIRC (still guarded for humans, ASM-0047) -> knull adjudicates what any lift is evidence OF. Only the last two move the kernel-content claim itself.",

Direction is consistent with the design's motivating suspicion (the non-blind agent pass was the weakest link) but attribution is unattributable in-design [EXTRAPOLATION: ASM-0543]. registry/assessments/nsk1-bprime2.json:78:      "recommended": "STAGE-1 FREE-GENERATION KEYED-RESCUE PROBE FIRST (rank 1), then the redesigned DRAFT record. The single largest inferential gap between this result and anything the flagship can use is teacher-forced-margin -> free-generation behaviour AT THE MEASURED KEYING CELLS - a gap this run cannot close because its generation secondaries were pivot-fixed at a dead cell. The designer should spec a bounded successor at cells (12,16) and (16,16) only (multiplicity 2, no new grid search): keyed emission + rescue-of-text-only-failures under real vs deranged-coin arms in free generation, an alpha ladder BELOW 1.0 (the keying-vs-damage frontier - does keying survive at alpha where the 13-16% plus-sign breakage does not?), >= 2 additional derangement seeds (single-seed robustness is the cheapest remaining attack), and the role arm carried as covariate. Same instrument family, same caps class (~USD 1-5 at measured throughput), phase:exploratory, fork-deciding only. WHY THIS ORDER: a rescue-positive makes the redesigned nsk1 record's rung logic and power real instead of extrapolated from margins; a rescue-negative (content readable but never integrated in generation) redirects Stage-1 toward read-out placement rather than a doomed campaign - either way the DRAFT record is written once, on measurements.",
registry/assessments/nsk1-bprime2.json:79:      "alternatives_deprioritised": "Multi-cell-robustness as a standalone (folded into rank 1 - the seeds and alpha ladder ARE the robustness buy; a wider grid re-search is not needed when 7 of 9 cells are cleanly dead twice over); scale successor (premature - no integration reading, and scale spend on an echo-grade channel is exactly the extrapolation-as-premise failure mode the honesty rules block); promotion to a frozen record now (blocked by the spec's own text - freezing happens only through a redesigned nsk1 record with gates and a skeptic memo, never off a Stage-0 diagnostic; and the skeptic memo must attack the two-contact corpus discipline, which is easier to defend WITH a Stage-1 rescue reading in hand).",

registry/assessments/a5-llm.json:52:      "[BINDING-NOTE — ASM-0012 vs GPT-5.6 item 5, RECONCILED] No contradiction, by direction: ASM-0012 stipulates the pro-LLM-lenient instrument is CONSERVATIVE for engine-superiority readings — true, and that is the direction that realized. GPT-5.6 correctly identifies the same leniency as ANTI-CONSERVATIVE for the differentiator-KILL direction (a vague-answer-accepting extractor could falsely place an LLM cell within 0.05). That failure mode had no purchase here: the kill did not fire and is 0.41 absolute out of reach even under the joint worst-case leniency+truncation repair bound (0.538 vs window 0.95). HOWEVER: had the kill fired, this record's instrument could NOT have validated it — a fired kill under a lenient-only extractor is uninterpretable. Binding on successors (a5-llm-frontier, a5-nl, l3a-cost, any record whose kill could plausibly fire): adopt the extractor sensitivity/specificity gate against blinded labels with unparsed-counted-wrong (GPT-5.6 G5(ii)) BEFORE freeze. Adopting it into a5-llm now is unlawful (frozen, run, unblinded — see the correction record) and unnecessary (the direction it protects did not realize)."

(2) PAIR, NOT PANEL — the cross-family pair is stronger than a single-family pair (kappa carries cross-family diagnostic content) but both judges are web-scale-trained LLMs with shared exposure to ordinary-language usage data; kappa is judge-pair stability, never inter-HUMAN-annotator agreement evidence; judge-pB's vendor family overlaps the materials' authoring agents (disclosed). The violation rates here ARE two LLMs' blind judgments of ordinary usage and literal condition satisfaction — NOT native-speaker human competence, which is the HS3 estimand. Binding weakenings: (1) ESTIMAND GAP — LLM ordinary-usage and literal-reading judgments approximate human judgments with unproven systematic offsets (ASM-0532); the pass-B literalism channel can push the necessity rate UP (hyper-literal condition failures) or DOWN (charitable reading), direction unproven, so NEITHER verdict direction is a measurement of the human rates. (2) PAIR, NOT PANEL — the cross-family pair is stronger than a single-family pair (kappa carries cross-family diagnostic content) but both judges are web-scale-trained LLMs with shared exposure to ordinary-language usage data; kappa is judge-pair stability, never inter-HUMAN-annotator agreement evidence; judge-pB's vendor family overlaps the materials' authoring agents (disclosed). (3) BRACKET RULE, NOT THE HUMAN COMBINATION RULE — the human record's combination rule is deliberately open; this record's pre-declared concordant/union bracket (ASM-0531) makes each verdict direction conservative under ITS least favourable rule, and the eventual human round is free to fix its own rule — the proxy consumes no open decision. (4) TRADITION FAMILIARITY — both judge families plausibly contain NSM-adjacent literature (ASM-0552); the mismatched-conditions probe bounds only crude non-literal endorsement. (5) LICENSE OF A PASS — a PASS-analog means exactly: 'a blind cross-family LLM pair, under the frozen two-pass rendering with working escapes, finds union-rule necessity violations at <= the 0.10 Wilson bar on the 200 pinned instances'. It is a WEAK FEASIBILITY PROXY (ASM-0550). It does NOT adjudicate HS3, does NOT resolve HS2, does NOT un-prune or prune g5, does NOT demote or confirm Pi as more than lint, does NOT substitute for the GATE-H human annotation, and does NOT extend to human annotators, other concepts, other instance distributions, or any coverage-general claim (kernel-expressibility coverage 0.3542 at rung molecules-v0, MEASURED by m0b on one incomplete kernel-v0 instance, restated mandatorily). (6) ASYMMETRIC READING — the disclosed channels have UNPROVEN direction here (unlike the endorsement-style records), so the bracket rule carries the conservatism instead: a FAIL-analog (concordant LB > 0.10 despite the lower bracket) is a strong early warning — pause Pi-hardening investment and escalate — while a PASS-analog licenses only continued investment (human annotator sourcing, Pi-line engineering) and never upgrades any claim. This mirrors the frozen g3 kill ('necessity failures >10% => defeasible-script stands, HS2 auto-resolves sidecar-only') in FORM ONLY; it can neither fire nor discharge g3's own kill — HS2 auto-resolution, g5 pruning and Pi demotion can be triggered ONLY by the human-annotated g3.     20	interpretive assessment (`registry/assessments/f2b-replicate.json`,
    21	does_not_license): **content-specificity ≠ ground-truth independence**. The
    22	verifier accepts iff the answer string-equals the canonical record, and
    23	d-qa/d-qa-r gold answers were **DEFINED by that same equality**. Under BOTH
    24	the real-semantics reading AND the definitional-circularity reading, a
    25	derangement destroys the lift — so no control run against self-defined gold
    26	can separate them. The two live hypotheses:
    27	
    28	- **H-TRANSFER (real content):** the kernel records carry semantic content
    29	  that is true of the covered concepts as externally understood; steering a
    30	  model toward record-consistent answers therefore raises accuracy against
    31	  ANY honest gold standard, not just the kernel's own.
    32	- **H-CIRC (circularity):** the records are an arbitrary-but-self-consistent
    33	  codebook; the lift exists only because gold was defined as codebook
    34	  content. Against externally-fixed gold, steering toward the codebook buys
    35	  nothing (and hurts wherever the codebook is wrong).
    36	
    37	Separating them requires items whose gold labels are fixed **independently of
    38	the kernel's string-equality** while the verifier remains **decidable** (able
    39	to engage) on them. Both properties at once are the whole design problem —
    40	see §2.
    41	
    42	## 2. Item source — and why the "public benchmark slice" form of RT-7a cannot adjudicate this
    43	
    44	**The deterministic verifier cannot engage with externally-authored text.**
    45	`KernelVerifier.check()` accepts a def-match/term-match option iff its text
    46	string-equals a canonical record's gloss, and a claim iff it string-equals a
    47	record segment (poc/f2b/runner/f2b_runner.py). Externally-authored option
    48	texts are never byte-equal to NSM-style canonical glosses, so on any public
    49	benchmark item the verifier either abstains off-coverage or rejects
    50	everything — in both cases the retry loop degenerates and no lift is
    51	mechanically possible **under either hypothesis**. This is not conjecture:
    52	
    53	- [MEASURED] In F2, `ext_vector()` bypassed the pipeline entirely — the
    54	  RT-7a secondary compared alone-vs-alone (p = 1.0 vacuously;
    55	  poc/f2/f2b-reanalysis.md §3, code cause named).
    56	- [MEASURED] In f2b-replicate the pipeline defect was fixed, and the d-ext
    57	  (OpenBookQA) slice ran through the verify arm's own retry pipeline — and
    58	  the verify-arm external vectors are **byte-identical** to the model-alone
    59	  external vectors at every seed (acc 0.242 = 0.242, 0 flips, seeds 0/1/2;
    60	  results-log/f2b-replicate.jsonl), because all 500 d-ext items are
    61	  `kernel_checkable = false` by construction and the verifier abstains on
    62	  100% of them. The instrument ran and was *degenerate by item construction*,
    63	  exactly as the frozen record's assumption predicted.
    64	
    65	So a run on OpenBookQA/MMLU-style items would produce a no-lift outcome that
    66	is **over-determined** — attributable to checkability/coverage failure just
    67	as well as to circularity — and would adjudicate nothing. Right-sizing
    68	demands we not spend on an arm whose outcome is fixed in advance under both
    69	hypotheses [STIPULATED design ruling; the two MEASURED bullets above are its
    70	evidence].
    71	
    72	**What breaks the circularity instead: externally-ADJUDICATED gold over
    73	kernel-checkable items.** The circularity confound lives in one specific
    74	place: *who fixes the gold label*. On d-qa/d-qa-r, gold := canonical-record
    75	content — gold and verifier-acceptance are the same function by definition.
    76	f2b-transfer keeps the item surface kernel-checkable (so the verifier
    77	demonstrably engages — gated, §6) and replaces the gold-definition step with
    78	**blind human adjudication**: judges who have never read the kernel records
    79	answer each item from their own competence with the covered everyday
    80	concepts (bird, eat, sleep, water, …), with a mandatory "cannot-say /
    81	none-of-these" escape. External gold is then fixed by ordinary speakers'
    82	understanding of the concepts — the same external authority that fixes the
    83	answer keys of crowd-authored benchmarks — and NOT by the kernel:
    84	
    85	- Under H-TRANSFER, blind judges endorse the kernel's content (they pick the
    86	  concept's own gloss in def/term-match items; they affirm own-record claims
    87	  and reject donor claims), so external gold ≈ membership gold and the
    88	  verifier lift survives re-scoring.
    89	- Under H-CIRC, an arbitrary codebook's glosses do not describe the concepts;
    90	  judges pick "cannot-say" or scatter (all options are kernel-rendered, so
    91	  register/style cannot cue the "intended" answer — style is constant across
    92	  options and across claim polarities by construction), external gold
    93	  decouples from membership gold, and steering toward the codebook cannot
    94	  raise external-gold accuracy.
    95	
    96	The decisive quantity has two layers, and the design measures both:
    97	
    98	1. **Stage 1 — endorsement (adjudication, ~$0 GPU):** A = share of resolved
    99	   adjudications where blind external gold equals membership gold. H-CIRC
   100	   predicts A near option-chance/claim-base-rate; H-TRANSFER predicts A high.
   101	   A pre-registered kill (§7 kill d) fires on low A **before any GPU spend**.
   102	2. **Stage 2 — system transfer (GPU, ~$1–3):** the arms of f2b-replicate
   103	   (minus the settled ones) re-run FRESH on the externally-labelled items,
   104	   every cell dual-scored against external and membership gold. The primary
   105	   is the verifier lift **on external gold**. This is the deployment-facing
   106	   number: retry dynamics on the judge–kernel disagreement set are not a
   107	   pure function of A, and promotion of verifier-offload beyond the
   108	   self-authored slice requires the measured system-level result, not an
   109	   inference from A [STIPULATED: measure, don't extrapolate].
   110	
   111	**Scope honesty (what this does NOT cover):** the item *surface* stays
   112	templated definitional QA over the 108 covered concepts. f2b-transfer
   113	removes the gold-definition circularity — the confound f2b-replicate
   114	foregrounded — NOT the template-style or benchmark-ecology questions (those
   115	remain the separate f2b-template-style stub and the coverage-growth line;
   195	   does not describe this concept" expressible. A concordant escape verdict
   196	   makes the item CONTENT-UNDECIDED: it counts as **non-agreement** in the
   197	   endorsement statistic A and is excluded from the Stage-2 eval set — it is
   198	   evidence against endorsement, never an instrument event.
   199	4. **Resolution.** gold_ext(item) = the concordant judge-1/judge-2 label
   200	   (including concordant escape ⇒ undecided). Discordant pairs go to judge-3
   201	   blind; judge-3's label resolves. Still-unresolvable items (judge-3
   202	   escapes where the pair split label-vs-label) are UNRESOLVED — instrument
   203	   events, capped by gate G-adj below.
   204	5. **Order and immutability.** Adjudication completes and `d-adj-t` is
   205	   hash-pinned (ops amendment) BEFORE the Stage-1 record is appended and
   206	   before any GPU cell runs. Labels are never revisited after the pin; the
   207	   Stage-2 runner fail-closes on the pin (ERR_PIN).
   208	6. **Effort** [STIPULATED estimate]: 360 items × ~20 s ≈ 2 h per judge;
   209	   tie-breaks ~15 min; LLM-judge fallback ≈ $1–3 API spend. Within O-3
   210	   patterns (D-IR-N blind adjudication precedent).
   211	7. **Adjudication standards (pre-data clarification; correction 1).** Added
   212	   by `registry/corrections/f2b-transfer/1-prefreeze-correction.json` BEFORE
   213	   any adjudication datum existed (judge-1 not started, judge-2 not run);
   214	   items 1–6 above and the blind item bytes are unchanged. These standards
   215	   define "correctly gives the meaning" (MCQ) and "true of X" (claims) for
   216	   both judges under ONE protocol. Each is the principled reading of
   217	   ordinary-understanding endorsement, chosen so the escape fires exactly
   218	   when the kernel's content does not describe the concept (§2) — never to
   219	   tune A; the A-direction of every rule is disclosed at the end
   220	   [STIPULATED design rulings].
   221	   - **S1 — MCQ correctness = fit + identification.** An option correctly
   222	     gives the meaning iff (a) **fit**: everything it says fits the concept
   223	     as ordinarily understood, each clause read as a typical-case (generic)
   224	     characterization that honors its own hedges ("at some times", "can",
   225	     "many", "some"); AND (b) **identification**: taken as a whole it says
   226	     what the word means — the definitional core is present and picks out
   227	     this concept rather than a different one. A clause TRUE of the concept
   228	     but not needed to define it NEVER disqualifies (surplus truth is not
   229	     error: the kernel's NSM explication style carries prototypical
   230	     non-essential clauses by design, so rejecting them would measure
   231	     lexicographic minimality — a style question §2 scopes OUT — not
   232	     content). A clause FALSE of the concept under the typical-case reading
   233	     ALWAYS disqualifies. Degrees: minor or even dominant surplus with the
   234	     core present → still correct; a pile of true facts that never says what
   235	     the thing is → fails (b) → not correct; any misfitting clause → not
   236	     correct.
   237	   - **S2 — best fit before NONE.** If more than one option passes S1, the
   238	     judge picks the one that best and most specifically gives the meaning
   239	     of the asked word itself, not of a related word (the event of dying is
   240	     not the meaning of "dead"). NONE iff no option passes S1 or the judge
   241	     cannot decide; both readings are one token (§4.3), optionally
   242	     distinguished in the comment field.
   243	   - **S3 — term-match mirror.** The judge picks the word an ordinary
   244	     speaker could be defining with the stem text (the S1 test in reverse;
   245	     extra true clauses in the stem do not break fit). A parenthetical
   246	     attached to a headword or option word ("find (X finds Y)", "right (of a
   247	     doable something)", "kind (gufo:Kind, sortal type)") only disambiguates
   248	     which sense/use of the word is meant; unfamiliar notation inside it is
   249	     ignored beyond sense-picking and is never itself a ground for NONE. If
   250	     the stem fits none of the four words → NONE; if it loosely fits two →
   251	     the better fit.
   252	   - **S4 — claims are judged at the generic standard, about what X means.**
   253	     "According to the definition of X, is S true of X?" asks whether S is
   254	     part of, or directly follows from, what X means as ordinarily
   255	     understood — including X's typical-case picture. **yes**: S holds in
   256	     the normal case / of normal instances ("birds fly"-style; exceptions do
   257	     not make it false), or S's own hedge holds. **no**: S misdescribes X
   258	     (not true even as a typical-case characterization; true only rarely or
   259	     accidentally), OR S has nothing to do with what X means — a statement
   260	     that X's definition neither says nor implies is answered no, including
   261	     statements so generic they say nothing about X in particular. **cannot
   262	     say**: inability only — S cannot be understood well enough to judge
   263	     even charitably, or the judge genuinely cannot decide; never merely
   264	     because S is oddly worded, partial, or only typically true, and never
   265	     as a soft no.
   266	   - **S5 — fragments, deixis, participants.** Claim statements are
   267	     fragments extracted from a longer description: unresolved "this
   268	     someone / this something / these parts / it", stray quote marks
   269	     (quoted-thought fragments), and bracketed clarifiers ("[the bookmark]")
   270	     are read charitably as pieces of a description of X's normal scenario;
   271	     the judged question is whether the fragment could belong to describing
   272	     X. X/Y letters name the participants given with the word's own title
   273	     ("break (X breaks Y)": X breaks, Y gets broken); a statement whose X/Y
   274	     match nothing in what the asked word means is not true of it → no.
   275	   - **S6 — register immunity (reading key).** The deliberately simplified
   276	     register is never a ground for NONE / no / cannot-say: "something of
   277	     kind K" reads as "a K" ("a something of kind event" = "an event";
   278	     "somethings of kind take happen" = "acts of taking happen"). Odd
   279	     grammar is simplification, not error and not a trick. The key is
   280	     constant across all items, options, and claim polarities, so it carries
   281	     zero bits about any answer (§2 style-constancy argument unchanged).
   282	   - **S7 — item independence.** Identical statements recur under different
   283	     words; each item is judged only against its own word, never by pattern,
   284	     recall, or reuse of an earlier answer.
   285	   - **A-direction disclosure [STIPULATED, honest].** Relative to the terse
   286	     pre-clarification judge wording ("close but not quite right → NONE"),
   287	     S1, S4 and S6 RAISE A by removing minimality- and style-driven escapes
   288	     that would fire under BOTH hypotheses (pure noise: under H-CIRC the
   289	     clauses are false/misfitting and S1(a)/S4-no still force the escape or
   290	     the disagreeing label, so no H-CIRC signal is lost). S4's
   291	     irrelevance→no clause raises agreement on donor claims under H-TRANSFER
   292	     AND lowers A under H-CIRC (arbitrary own-record content reads as
   293	     no-against-membership-yes) — sharpening kill (d) in both directions.
   294	     Two honest costs are accepted rather than patched: (i) contentless
   295	     own-record segments lose credit (no / cannot-say against
   440	  — an order of magnitude inside the <$100 auto-authorization envelope,
   441	  and the design can FAIL for ~$0 at Stage 1.
   442	- Agent effort: builder (authored and committed pre-freeze; §3.1 plan) +
   443	  adjudication tooling + runner adaptation of poc/f2b (drop arms, add dual
   444	  scoring + engagement block) ≈ 1–2 agent-days.
   445	
   446	## 10. Decision table (design-time forecast; the assessment owns post-hoc meaning)
   447	
   448	| outcome | mechanical verdict | what it adjudicates |
   449	|---|---|---|
   450	| A low (kill d) | FAIL, $0 GPU | blind judges do not endorse the kernel's content: H-CIRC supported at this kernel instance; verifier-offload promotion beyond self-authored slices is blocked |
   451	| A high, primary + controls pass | PASS | the lift survives ground-truth-independent gold and stays content-specific and better-than-commodity: H-TRANSFER supported within §8's envelope; the circularity confound named by f2b-replicate is closed |
   452	| A high, primary fails | FAIL (kill a) | content externally endorsed but the SYSTEM lift does not transfer — indicts retry dynamics on the disagreement set, not (necessarily) record content; assessment decides the follow-up |
   453	| shuffled recovers ≥30% on ext gold | FAIL (kill b) | any external-gold lift was structure, not content |
   454	| gloss arm closes as much at ≤ FLOPs | FAIL (kill c) | commodity verification suffices on honest gold |
   455	| engagement/headroom/adjudication gates fail | INSTRUMENT-INVALID | instrument events; no hypothesis is read (the RT-7a lesson, enforced) |
   456	
   457	## 11. Execution checklist (post-sign-off; Opus runner, practices 1–5)
   458	
   459	1. Commit `data/d-qa-t/build-dqat.py` (seed §3.1 verbatim); build; LC8-t
   460	   must pass fail-closed; `corpus-pin` d-qa-t.
   461	2. Write d-qa-t pin into the DRAFT record (replacing its PINNED-AT-INPUTS
   462	   placeholder pre-freeze, or by ops amendment post-freeze — either is
   463	   lawful pre-final-run; pre-freeze is cleaner), then
   464	   `prereg-freeze --experiment f2b-transfer`; post the frozen_sha256 to the
   465	   coordination issue (RT-15).
   466	3. Run §4 adjudication; assemble d-adj-t; `corpus-pin`; ops amendment fills
   467	   the d-adj-t placeholder; append the Stage-1 adjudication-instrument
   468	   final-phase record via log-append.
   469	4. Run verdict-gen: if FAIL (kill d) → stop, Codex audit, done at ~$0. If
   470	   INCOMPLETE-DATA (stage-1 passed) → proceed.
   471	5. Pre-spend: `reuse-check` (record output; pre-committed fresh-runs
   472	   decision), dry-plan vs caps, mock run of the adapted runner
   473	   (poc/f2b-transfer; harness_manifest ops-amended in).
   474	6. Stage-2 GPU run (single A100/A10G, foreground gates, concurrency cap 5);
   475	   append final-phase records; verdict-gen; Codex GATE-A audit; then FABLE

item.text:
Not sound to commit as-is. The central mechanical result is reported correctly, and the main scope boundary is understood, but several landmark-level phrasings overstate what Stage 1 established.

## Three most important concerns, ranked

1. **H-CIRC is described too strongly as “REFUTED” or “killed.”**  
   [Lines 39–41](</home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/interpretations/f2b-transfer-stage1.md:39>) say its prediction is “REFUTED,” the codebook “does not survive,” and Stage 1 “kills” content-level circularity. The frozen H-CIRC is explicitly disjunctive: low endorsement **and/or** no external-gold lift. Stage 1 rejects its low-endorsement branch and removes the gold-definition leg for this instance/slice; it does not refute H-CIRC overall. The following paragraph acknowledges this, but does not undo the overclaim. Prefer: “strongly disconfirms the arbitrary-codebook/content-level branch at this instance and covered slice.”

2. **The result is repeatedly presented as human-fixed gold rather than dual-judge, human-anchored gold.**  
   [Lines 39, 43, 53, and 77](</home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/interpretations/f2b-transfer-stage1.md:39>) use phrases such as “a kernel-naive human … independently reproduced,” “external gold authority—ordinary human understanding,” and “an authority it did not define—a blind kernel-naive human.” But inclusion in the 324 resolved set is conditional on concordance with GPT-5.5. The human contribution is real and present in every resolved label, but the authority is the frozen dual-judge procedure, not the human alone. Use “blind external dual-judge gold, human-anchored by judge-1.”

3. **The capstone path is falsely made binary and prematurely “compute-blocked only.”**  
   [Line 79](</home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/interpretations/f2b-transfer-stage1.md:79>) says Stage 2 is compute-blocked only, although judge-3, pinning, record append, and verdict generation remain mandatory. [Line 80](</home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/interpretations/f2b-transfer-stage1.md:80>) says one GPU run will produce either the first independent end-task PASS or a sharp FAIL; the registered outcome space also includes INSTRUMENT-INVALID, NULL, and INCONCLUSIVE, plus kills (b)/(c) that do not simply adjudicate residual H-CIRC. The closing claim that Stage 2 “decides H-TRANSFER vs residual H-CIRC” repeats this overcompression.

## Quantitative audit

The primary mechanical quantities match [stage1-analysis.json](</home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/poc/f2b-transfer/judge-1-results/stage1-analysis.json>):

- 324/360 concordance = 0.90.
- 36/360 unresolved = 10%.
- `n_undecided = 0`.
- 317/324 = 0.978395.
- One-sided 95% Wilson LB = 0.960620.
- `adjudication_valid = true`.
- `stage1_endorsement_fail = false`.

One quantitative error remains: [line 29](</home/ec2-user/css/kernel/kernel-of-truth/docs/next/interpretations/f2b-transfer-stage1.md:29>) gives the worst-case 317/360 Wilson LB as “≈0.84.” Under the document’s declared **one-sided 95%** convention it is approximately **0.850**, rounding to 0.85. Approximately 0.843 is the two-sided 95% Wilson lower endpoint. More importantly, this robustness computation is absent from `stage1-analysis.json`, despite being tagged as a coordinator-supplied MEASURED result and “never recomputed.”

The cost claims are consistent with the preregistration: approximately $0.55 point estimate, $1.10 worst-case plan, $15/4 GPU-hour caps.

## Capstone calibration

The key verdict discipline is correct:

- The experiment remains at the lawful Stage-1 intermediate state; no experiment PASS exists.
- No external-gold verifier lift has been measured.
- The efficiency and correctness theses remain **INCONCLUSIVE-PENDING**.
- Coverage, benchmark ecology, NL reachability, PRM-class, host-scale, and slope claims are correctly withheld.
- The result is correctly recognized as a strong positive on the **gold-authority/content-endorsement axis**, not the input/reachability axis.

However, “this measured whether the content is TRUE by outside lights—yes” in line 77 should become “whether it is externally endorsed under this frozen protocol—yes.” “True” widens a protocol-bound endorsement statistic into an ontological claim.

The correctness-thesis paragraph at line 81 should also be trimmed or corrected: Stage 2 is not a “full human adjudicator,” judge-3 only resolves discordances, and the g3-proxy comparison introduces an additional result not grounded by the document’s declared source list.

## Tag errors

- **Line 29:** worst-case bound is DERIVED/ANALYTIC, not MEASURED from the cited artifact.
- **Line 31:** INCOMPLETE-DATA is the registered expected lawful state, but the formal record append and verdict generation have not occurred. Tag it STIPULATED/DERIVED, not as an already-issued MEASURED experiment state.
- **Lines 39–41:** “REFUTED,” “kills,” and “does not survive” are interpretive inferences, not STIPULATED facts.
- **Line 53:** “ordinary human understanding” mischaracterizes a mixed human/LLM adjudication procedure.
- **Line 77:** “TRUE by outside lights” is an extrapolative interpretation and is too strong even under that tag.
